last_next_action.reason: execution queue is empty; wake the agent to synthesize or update the research agenda and advance all currently runnable safe project work from objective, evidence, human advice, and safety policy

recent_evidence.summary: c02 det=1 Ramsey 14-average autosave review: Bridge-free reviewed a late 14-average even autosave subset of running job nv23_ramsey_20260514_230820_auto_ramsey. The bridge remained running, later at 15/16 averages, with monitor active, no last_error, and stop_requested=false; latest read-only status at 02:32 EDT showed Final = 42.702 kcps. Scan-order-aware drift used snake order and found no flagged averages. Readout2/self-baseline target bins remain weak/provisional: direct 13C 0.19%, det-13C 0.79%, carrier 0.41%, det+13C 0.82%, previous 1.9 MHz ambiguity 0.48%. No T2star or 13C claim; wait for terminal 16-average data or hard anomaly evidence.

Terminal 16-average det=1.0 MHz Ramsey/T2star/13C discriminator completed successfully. Raw export and scan-order-aware drift review verified ramsey.xml, tau 0..10 us, 51 points, 16x50000 reps, snake order, no flagged averages, pre/post counts 40.445/42.702 kcps, no stop request, safe shutdown ok. Readout2/self-baseline target FFT amplitudes: direct 13C 0.14%, det-13C 0.73%, carrier 0.41%, det+13C 0.84%, previous 1.9 MHz 0.56%. The det+13C-compatible component is a weak candidate but incomplete; T2* is short/few-us order only, not a final scalar; 13C is not yet well-supported.

Bridge-free weak-signal follow-up synthesis after terminal det=1.0 Ramsey. Compared det=1.5 and det=1.0 Ramsey plus terminal weak-pi pODMR. T2star remains short/few-us order but scalar-sensitive. 13C is not well-supported yet, but the high-sideband-compatible component is a plausible weak candidate because it moves with programmed det; planned a targeted det=1.25 MHz Ramsey artifact-rejection discriminator with explicit model targets 0.865/1.250/1.635 MHz and 800k shots/point, pending verifier/advisory gates.

Targeted third-det Ramsey follow-up at det=1.25 MHz was verified, advisory-checked, and launched. Pre-enqueue advisory ok=true with no blockers; estimated 692.6 s per average under the 900 s nighttime cap. Running bridge job nv23_ramsey_20260515_030822_auto_ramsey is active at average 1/16, monitor active with no last_error, stop_requested=false, Final text about 43.7 kcps, queued/staging otherwise empty. This touched the bridge queue; no further bridge mutation until terminal/anomaly evidence.

c02 det=1 terminal review and det=1.25 follow-up launched: Terminal 16-average det=1.0 MHz Ramsey completed cleanly (pre/post counts 40.445/42.702 kcps, no stop request, no drift flags). Readout2/self-baseline target bins: direct 13C 0.14%, det-13C 0.73%, carrier 0.41%, det+13C 0.84%, previous 1.9 MHz 0.56%. This supports short/few-us T2star order but not a final scalar; 13C remains weak/incomplete. Because the high-sideband-compatible component shifted with det from the prior det=1.5 run, a targeted third-det Ramsey at det=1.25 MHz was modeled, verifier/advisory passed, and job nv23_ramsey_20260515_030822_auto_ramsey is now running (avg 1/16, Final about 43.7 kcps, monitor active, no stop request). No further bridge mutation while it runs.

last_human_request.preview: Objective: Find a magnetic-field-aligned NV from image172647, then obtain a well-supported T2star and 13C conclusion. Start a project using the spatial range of <MATLAB_23C_ROOT>\SavedImages\3DXYZ-Image-2026-05-14-172647.mat as the initial search region. Identify an NV aligned to the magnetic field within that image range; if none is found, search different regions. Judge magnetic-field alignment using a strong-pi pulsed ODMR scan; if it does not show a clear usable resonance, reject
